questionText: Choose the correct result for this bunny play mat puzzle: the mat is a rectangle 12 cm by 8 cm, but a 3 cm by 4 cm corner is cut away. Then 10 cm² of the mat is covered by toy blocks. How much mat is still showing?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can calculate the area of rectangles and composite shapes using counting and formula
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Measurement & Conversion: Perimeter & Area, aligned to Students can calculate the area of rectangles and composite shapes using counting and formula.
options: [{"text":"74 cm²","correct":true},{"text":"86 cm²","correct":false},{"text":"94 cm²","correct":false},{"text":"62 cm²","correct":false}]
explanation: Great job thinking in steps! The full rectangle is 12 × 8 = 96 cm². The missing corner is 3 × 4 = 12 cm², so the mat area is 96 - 12 = 84 cm². Then 10 cm² is covered, so 84 - 10 = 74 cm². Some wrong answers come from forgetting the cut-out or subtracting the wrong part.

questionText: Which method correctly solves this zoo sign problem: the sign is an L-shape made from a 9 cm by 5 cm rectangle and a 4 cm by 2 cm rectangle joined with no overlap, and then 6 cm² are covered by animal stickers?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can calculate the area of rectangles and composite shapes using counting and formula
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Measurement & Conversion: Perimeter & Area, aligned to Students can calculate the area of rectangles and composite shapes using counting and formula.
options: [{"text":"Add the rectangle areas, 45 + 8 = 53, then subtract 6 to get 47 cm² uncovered.","correct":true},{"text":"Subtract the rectangle areas, 45 - 8 = 37, then subtract 6 to get 31 cm² uncovered.","correct":false},{"text":"Add the side lengths, 9 + 5 + 4 + 2 = 20, then subtract 6 to get 14 cm² uncovered.","correct":false},{"text":"Multiply all the numbers, 9 × 5 × 4 × 2 = 360, then subtract 6 to get 354 cm² uncovered.","correct":false}]
explanation: Fantastic work! The two rectangles are both parts of the sign, so add their areas: 45 + 8 = 53 cm². Then subtract the 6 cm² covered by stickers. That leaves 47 cm² uncovered.

questionText: A snack tray is a rectangle that is 6 cm long and 3 cm wide. What is the area of the tray?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can calculate the area of rectangles and composite shapes using counting and formula

options: [{"text":"9 cm²","correct":false},{"text":"18 cm²","correct":true},{"text":"20 cm²","correct":false},{"text":"27 cm²","correct":false}]
explanation: The area of a rectangle is length × width. So, 6 × 3 = 18 cm². That is why 18 cm² is correct. 9 cm² is too small, 20 cm² does not match 6 × 3, and 27 cm² would come from 9 × 3, not this rectangle.

questionText: Which method correctly solves this bunny play mat puzzle: the mat is made from a 10 cm by 6 cm rectangle with a 3 cm by 2 cm corner cut out, and then 8 cm² of the mat is covered by toy blocks? Choose the answer that gives the correct clean area and the right reason.
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can calculate the area of rectangles and composite shapes using counting and formula
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Measurement & Conversion: Perimeter & Area, aligned to Students can calculate the area of rectangles and composite shapes using counting and formula.
options: [{"text":"Start with 10 × 6 = 60 cm², subtract the missing corner 3 × 2 = 6 cm² to get 54 cm², then subtract the 8 cm² covered by toy blocks to get 46 cm² clean area.","correct":true},{"text":"Add all the numbers 10 + 6 + 3 + 2 + 8 to get 29 cm² clean area because area uses all the side lengths and extra pieces.","correct":false},{"text":"Start with 10 × 6 = 60 cm², then add the corner 3 × 2 = 6 cm² and subtract 8 cm² to get 58 cm² clean area because the corner is part of the mat shape.","correct":false},{"text":"Subtract side lengths first: 10 - 3 = 7 and 6 - 2 = 4, then do 7 × 4 = 28 cm², and subtract 8 cm² to get 20 cm² clean area.","correct":false}]
explanation: Great thinking! The big rectangle is 60 cm². The cut-out corner is missing, so take away 6 cm² to get 54 cm². Then 8 cm² is covered, so 54 - 8 = 46 cm². The other answers mix up area with side lengths or add the missing corner instead of subtracting it.
